# Lockout / Tagout for Feed Pump 101A

Document ID: SOP-LOTO-4A-017  
Revision: 2  
Effective date: 2026-04-15  
Applies to: `P-101A`, `P-101B`, `V-204`, `TK-12`

## Purpose

Provide a safe isolation method before maintenance on Feed Pump 101A or its associated discharge valve.

## References

- OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147
- Site Energy Isolation Standard EIS-04
- Unit 4A P&amp;ID feed system overview

## Required PPE

- Hard hat
- Chemical splash goggles
- Cut-resistant gloves
- Hearing protection

## Procedure

1. Notify the board operator that Pump 101A will be removed from service.
2. Confirm standby unit `P-101B` is available if process continuity is required.
3. Close upstream suction source from `TK-12` as directed by operations.
4. Shut down `P-101A` from the local starter and verify zero rotation.
5. Close discharge isolation valve `V-204`.
6. Apply lock and tag at the motor disconnect for `P-101A`.
7. Bleed trapped pressure from the pump casing and verify the drain runs clear.
8. Attempt local start to confirm zero energy state.
9. Record the isolation in the shift log and maintenance permit.

## Cautions

- If the work scope includes seal replacement, flush connections must be depressurized before loosening tubing.
- Any leakage to the oily water drain during isolation must be reported per Environmental Work Practice EWP-11.
